<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Makoto no Michi]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Makoto no Michi]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Makoto no Michi]] {{Short description|Japanese new religion}} {{Infobox religion | icon = | icon_width = | icon_alt = | name = Makoto no Michi | native_name = [[真の道]] | native_name_lang = ja | image = Makoto-no-michi HQ, Setagaya.jpg | imagewidth = | alt = | caption = Makoto no Michi's headquarters | abbreviation = | type = | main_classification = | orientation = | scripture = ''Makoto no Michi shinji'' (真の道神示) (2 volumes) | theology = | polity = | governance = | structure = | leader_title = | leader_name = | leader_title1 = | leader_name1 = | leader_title2 = | leader_name2 = | leader_title3 = | leader_name3 = | fellowships_type = | fellowships = | fellowships_type1 = | fellowships1 = | division_type = | division = | division_type1 = | division1 = | division_type2 = | division2 = | division_type3 = | division3 = | associations = | full_communion = | area = | language = | liturgy = | headquarters = [[Kasuya, Tokyo]] | territory = | possessions = | founder = Hagiwara Makoto ([[:ja:萩原真|萩原真]]) | founded_date = 1949 | founded_place = | independence = | reunion = | recognition = | separated_from = | branched_from = | merger = | absorbed = | separations = | merged_into = | defunct = | congregations_type = | congregations = | members = <!-- or | number_of_followers = --> | ministers_type = | ministers = | missionaries = | churches = | missionary organization = | aid = | hospitals = | nursing_homes = | primary_schools = | secondary_schools = | tax_status = | tertiary = | seminaries = | other_names = | publications = | website = {{URL|https://www.makoto.or.jp/}} | website_title1 = | slogan = | logo = | module = | footnotes = }} {{nihongo|'''Makoto no Michi'''|[[真の道]]}} ({{lit|The Sincere Path}}) is a [[Shinto]]-based [[Japanese new religion]]. The headquarters of Makoto no Michi is located in [[Kasuya, Tokyo]]. [[misogi|Waterfall meditation]], which is also a [[shugendo]] practice, is one of its main practices.<ref>{{cite web | title=滝行に参加してみたい方へのご案内 | website=宗教法人真の道 - 那須に中心の宮を置く古神道 | date=2019-02-25 | url=https://www.makoto.or.jp/archives/120/ | language=ja | access-date=2025-05-06}}</ref>
==History== In 1948, the religion was founded as '''Chidori-kai''' (千鳥会). Hagiwara Makoto ([[:ja:萩原真|萩原真]], 1910–1981), who had returned from China after World War II, registered it as a religious organization in 1949 and became its leader. In 1952, the organization's name was changed to Makoto no Michi.<ref>''Shinshūkyō no sekai'' / 新宗教の世界. 大蔵出版, 1978.</ref><ref name="history">{{cite web | title=教え・歴史 | website=宗教法人真の道 - 那須に中心の宮を置く古神道 | date=2018-10-26 | url=https://www.makoto.or.jp/history/ | language=ja | access-date=2025-05-06}}</ref>
One of [[Hirohito]]'s former aides claimed that [[Empress Nagako]]'s [[lady-in-waiting]] Imaki Yoshiko ([[:ja:今城誼子|今城誼子]]) was a follower of Makoto no Michi, and that the empress herself may have been influenced by the religion as she was looking for a way to alleviate Hirohito's illness during his later years.<ref>{{cite web | title=皇后さま、新興宗教の“教祖”に心酔!? 宮内庁が問題視した「教団」と、皇室の知られざる事実 | website=サイゾーウーマン | date=2021-03-19 | url=https://cyzowoman.jp/2021/03/post_326854_1.html | language=ja | access-date=2025-05-06}}</ref>
==Spiritual leaders== In order of succession, the spiritual leaders (教え主) of Makoto no Michi are:<ref name="history"/>
#Hagiwara Makoto ([[:ja:萩原真|萩原真]]): founder and leader from the religion's founding until his death in 1981 #Hagiwara Shinmei ([[:ja:萩原真明|萩原真明]]): son of Hagiwara Makoto and leader from February 1982 to May 2023 #Nagasawa Meishin (永澤明眞): leader from May 2024 to present
==Beliefs and practices== ===Divine revelations=== Makoto no Michi practices the receiving of {{nihongo|divine revelations|神示|shinji}} (お伝え), in which a {{nihongo|spirit medium|神伝え人|}} under divine possession works together with a spirit mediator known as a {{nihongo|''saniwa''|審神者}} (サニワ), along with various ritual assistants<ref>{{cite book |last=Hagiwara |first=Shinmei 萩原真明 |date=2000 |title=Makoto no Michi shinji (vol. 2) 真の道神示 第二集 |location=Tokyo |publisher=Makoto no Michi Publishing Department 真の道出版部 |page=292 |language=ja}}</ref> (''see also'' [[chinkon kishin]]).<ref>{{cite web | title=神示 | website=宗教法人真の道 - 那須に中心の宮を置く古神道 | date=2018-12-31 | url=https://www.makoto.or.jp/archives/64/ | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-03}}</ref>
===Geography=== Makoto no Michi's sacred geography is derived from that of [[Oomoto]] founder [[Onisaburo Deguchi]]'s ''[[Reikai Monogatari]]'', in which Japan is viewed as a {{nihongo|model|雛形|hinagata}} or microcosm of the world. [[Hokkaido]] is viewed as the equivalent of North America, [[Honshu]] as Eurasia, [[Shikoku]] as Australia, [[Kyushu]] as Africa, and [[Taiwan]] as South America; these equivalences stem from their common mythical origins during the creation of the world.<ref name="Shinji2">{{cite book |last=Hagiwara |first=Shinmei 萩原真明 |date=2000 |title=Makoto no Michi shinji (vol. 2) 真の道神示 第二集 |location=Tokyo |publisher=Makoto no Michi Publishing Department 真の道出版部 |pages=250-1 |language=ja}}</ref> The geographic equivalents of the main Japanese islands and Taiwan with the world's continents are identical in both Oomoto<ref>{{cite web | last=Roberts | first=Bill | title=Hokkaido Journal | website=大本公式サイト/ Oomoto Official Site | date=2010-05-20 | url=https://www.oomoto.or.jp/English/enVisitor/enBill7.html | access-date=2025-06-04}}</ref> and Makoto no Michi. For comparison, Oomoto's headquarters in [[Ayabe, Kyoto|Ayabe]] has a garden with islands that represent the same set of islands and continents.<ref>{{cite web | title=金竜海 | website=オニペディア | date=2019-11-18 | url=https://onipedia.info/wiki/%E9%87%91%E7%AB%9C%E6%B5%B7 | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-05}}</ref>
For mountains and lakes, equivalents include:<ref name="Shinji2"/> *''[[Hokkaido]] – [[North America]]'' **[[Daisetsuzan Volcanic Group|Daisetsuzan]] – [[Rocky Mountains]] *''[[Honshu]] – [[Eurasia]]'' **[[Lake Towada]] – [[Lake Baikal]] **[[Mount Gassan]] – [[Central Siberian Plateau]] **[[Lake Suwa]] – [[Lake Manasarovar]] / [[Mount Kailash]] **[[Mount Kisokoma]] – [[Mount Sinai]] **[[Mount Fuji]] – [[Himalayas]] / [[Mount Everest]] *''[[Shikoku]] – [[Australia (continent)|Australia]]'' **[[Mount Tsurugi (Tokushima)|Mount Tsurugi]] – [[Uluru]] *''[[Kyushu]] – [[Africa]]'' **[[Mount Aso]] – [[Kilimanjaro]]'' *''[[Taiwan]] – [[South America]]'' **[[Yu Shan]] – [[Aconcagua]]
==Locations== Makoto no Michi's administrative headquarters is in [[Kasuya, Tokyo]]. The religion also has a [[misogi|waterfall meditation]] site at Nigitama-no-miya (和魂宮; also called ''Tsuki-miya'' 月宮) just north of [[Mount Takao]]. Makoto no Michi's "inner temple" (''Oku-miya'' 奥宮) is Naohi-no-miya (直毘宮), located in [[Nasu, Tochigi]].<ref>{{cite web | title=概要・地図 | website=宗教法人真の道 - 那須に中心の宮を置く古神道 | date=2018-10-26 | url=https://www.makoto.or.jp/overview/ | language=ja | access-date=2025-05-06}}</ref>
==Books== Makoto no Michi has published about a dozen books, a few of which include:<ref>{{cite web | title=書籍 | website=宗教法人真の道 - 那須に中心の宮を置く古神道 | date=2018-10-26 | url=https://www.makoto.or.jp/book/ | language=ja | access-date=2025-05-06}}</ref>
*''Makoto no Michi shinji'' / 真の道神示 (1972) *''Makoto no Michi shinji: vol. 2'' / 真の道神示 第二集 (2000)
Other books include: *真を求めて:萩原真自伝 *飛翔:萩原真先生を偲ぶ *大峰老仙:わしはピヨピヨじゃったよ *守護霊様と私（第二集） *真手入門 *天源 *車に乗った巫女
Books in the ''Makoto no Michi o motomete'' (まことの道を求めて) series: #天命が見える #梶さんの霊界通信 #人間の幸福
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== *{{Official website|https://www.makoto.or.jp/}} {{in lang|ja}}
{{Japanese new religions}} [[en:Makoto no Michi]] {{wikidata link|Q134154094}} [[Category:1949 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Japanese new religions]] [[Category:Organizations based in Tokyo]] [[Category:Religious organizations based in Japan]] [[Category:Religious organizations established in 1949]] [[Category:Shinto new religious movements]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages]]

[[Hokkaido]] is viewed as the equivalent of North America, [[Honshu]] as Eurasia, [[Shikoku]] as Australia, [[Kyushu]] as Africa, and [[Taiwan]] as South America; these equivalences stem from their common mythical origins during the creation of the world.<ref name="Shinji2">{{cite book |last=Hagiwara |first=Shinmei 萩原真明 |date=2000 |title=Makoto no Michi shinji (vol. 2) 真の道神示 第二集 |location=Tokyo |publisher=Makoto no Michi Publishing Department 真の道出版部 |pages=250-1 |language=ja}}</ref> The geographic equivalents of the main Japanese islands and Taiwan with the world's continents are identical in both Oomoto<ref>{{cite web | last=Roberts | first=Bill | title=Hokkaido Journal | website=大本公式サイト/ Oomoto Official Site | date=2010-05-20 | url=https://www.oomoto.or.jp/English/enVisitor/enBill7.html | access-date=2025-06-04}}</ref> and Makoto no Michi. For comparison, Oomoto's headquarters in [[Ayabe, Kyoto|Ayabe]] has a garden with islands that represent the same set of islands and continents.<ref>{{cite web | title=金竜海 | website=オニペディア | date=2019-11-18 | url=https://onipedia.info/wiki/%E9%87%91%E7%AB%9C%E6%B5%B7 | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-05}}</ref>
For mountains and lakes, equivalents include:<ref name="Shinji2"/> *''[[Hokkaido]] – [[North America]]'' **[[Daisetsuzan Volcanic Group|Daisetsuzan]] – [[Rocky Mountains]] *''[[Honshu]] – [[Eurasia]]'' **[[Lake Towada]] – [[Lake Baikal]] **[[Mount Gassan]] – [[Central Siberian Plateau]] **[[Lake Suwa]] – [[Lake Manasarovar]] / [[Mount Kailash]] **[[Mount Kisokoma]] – [[Mount Sinai]] **[[Mount Fuji]] – [[Himalayas]] / [[Mount Everest]] *''[[Shikoku]] – [[Australia (continent)|Australia]]'' **[[Mount Tsurugi (Tokushima)|Mount Tsurugi]] – [[Uluru]] *''[[Kyushu]] – [[Africa]]'' **[[Mount Aso]] – [[Kilimanjaro]]'' *''[[Taiwan]] – [[South America]]'' **[[Yu Shan]] – [[Aconcagua]]
==Locations== Makoto no Michi's administrative headquarters is in [[Kasuya, Tokyo]]. The religion also has a [[misogi|waterfall meditation]] site at Nigitama-no-miya (和魂宮; also called ''Tsuki-miya'' 月宮) just north of [[Mount Takao]]. Makoto no Michi's "inner temple" (''Oku-miya'' 奥宮) is Naohi-no-miya (直毘宮), located in [[Nasu, Tochigi]].<ref>{{cite web | title=概要・地図 | website=宗教法人真の道 - 那須に中心の宮を置く古神道 | date=2018-10-26 | url=https://www.makoto.or.jp/overview/ | language=ja | access-date=2025-05-06}}</ref>
==Books== Makoto no Michi has published about a dozen books, a few of which include:<ref>{{cite web | title=書籍 | website=宗教法人真の道 - 那須に中心の宮を置く古神道 | date=2018-10-26 | url=https://www.makoto.or.jp/book/ | language=ja | access-date=2025-05-06}}</ref>
*''Makoto no Michi shinji'' / 真の道神示 (1972) *''Makoto no Michi shinji: vol. 2'' / 真の道神示 第二集 (2000)
Other books include: *真を求めて:萩原真自伝 *飛翔:萩原真先生を偲ぶ *大峰老仙:わしはピヨピヨじゃったよ *守護霊様と私（第二集） *真手入門 *天源 *車に乗った巫女
Books in the ''Makoto no Michi o motomete'' (まことの道を求めて) series: #天命が見える #梶さんの霊界通信 #人間の幸福
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== *{{Official website|https://www.makoto.or.jp/}} {{in lang|ja}}
{{Japanese new religions}} [[en:Makoto no Michi]] {{wikidata link|Q134154094}} [[Category:1949 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Japanese new religions]] [[Category:Organizations based in Tokyo]] [[Category:Religious organizations based in Japan]] [[Category:Religious organizations established in 1949]] [[Category:Shinto new religious movements]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Makoto no Michi]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Makoto no Michi]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Makoto no Michi]] {{Short description|Japanese new religion}} {{Infobox religion | icon = | icon_width = | icon_alt = | name = Makoto no Michi | native_name = [[真の道]] | native_name_lang = ja | image = Makoto-no-michi HQ, Setagaya.jpg | imagewidth = | alt = | caption = Makoto no Michi's headquarters | abbreviation = | type = | main_classification = | orientation = | scripture = ''Makoto no Michi shinji'' (真の道神示) (2 volumes) | theology = | polity = | governance = | structure = | leader_title = | leader_name = | leader_title1 = | leader_name1 = | leader_title2 = | leader_name2 = | leader_title3 = | leader_name3 = | fellowships_type = | fellowships = | fellowships_type1 = | fellowships1 = | division_type = | division = | division_type1 = | division1 = | division_type2 = | division2 = | division_type3 = | division3 = | associations = | full_communion = | area = | language = | liturgy = | headquarters = [[Kasuya, Tokyo]] | territory = | possessions = | founder = Hagiwara Makoto ([[:ja:萩原真|萩原真]]) | founded_date = 1949 | founded_place = | independence = | reunion = | recognition = | separated_from = | branched_from = | merger = | absorbed = | separations = | merged_into = | defunct = | congregations_type = | congregations = | members = <!-- or | number_of_followers = --> | ministers_type = | ministers = | missionaries = | churches = | missionary organization = | aid = | hospitals = | nursing_homes = | primary_schools = | secondary_schools = | tax_status = | tertiary = | seminaries = | other_names = | publications = | website = {{URL|https://www.makoto.or.jp/}} | website_title1 = | slogan = | logo = | module = | footnotes = }} {{nihongo|'''Makoto no Michi'''|[[真の道]]}} ({{lit|The Sincere Path}}) is a [[Shinto]]-based [[Japanese new religion]]. The headquarters of Makoto no Michi is located in [[Kasuya, Tokyo]]. [[misogi|Waterfall meditation]], which is also a [[shugendo]] practice, is one of its main practices.<ref>{{cite web | title=滝行に参加してみたい方へのご案内 | website=宗教法人真の道 - 那須に中心の宮を置く古神道 | date=2019-02-25 | url=https://www.makoto.or.jp/archives/120/ | language=ja | access-date=2025-05-06}}</ref>
==History== In 1948, the religion was founded as '''Chidori-kai''' (千鳥会). Hagiwara Makoto ([[:ja:萩原真|萩原真]], 1910–1981), who had returned from China after World War II, registered it as a religious organization in 1949 and became its leader. In 1952, the organization's name was changed to Makoto no Michi.<ref>''Shinshūkyō no sekai'' / 新宗教の世界. 大蔵出版, 1978.</ref><ref name="history">{{cite web | title=教え・歴史 | website=宗教法人真の道 - 那須に中心の宮を置く古神道 | date=2018-10-26 | url=https://www.makoto.or.jp/history/ | language=ja | access-date=2025-05-06}}</ref>
One of [[Hirohito]]'s former aides claimed that [[Empress Nagako]]'s [[lady-in-waiting]] Imaki Yoshiko ([[:ja:今城誼子|今城誼子]]) was a follower of Makoto no Michi, and that the empress herself may have been influenced by the religion as she was looking for a way to alleviate Hirohito's illness during his later years.<ref>{{cite web | title=皇后さま、新興宗教の“教祖”に心酔!? 宮内庁が問題視した「教団」と、皇室の知られざる事実 | website=サイゾーウーマン | date=2021-03-19 | url=https://cyzowoman.jp/2021/03/post_326854_1.html | language=ja | access-date=2025-05-06}}</ref>
==Spiritual leaders== In order of succession, the spiritual leaders (教え主) of Makoto no Michi are:<ref name="history"/>
#Hagiwara Makoto ([[:ja:萩原真|萩原真]]): founder and leader from the religion's founding until his death in 1981 #Hagiwara Shinmei ([[:ja:萩原真明|萩原真明]]): son of Hagiwara Makoto and leader from February 1982 to May 2023 #Nagasawa Meishin (永澤明眞): leader from May 2024 to present
==Beliefs and practices== ===Divine revelations=== Makoto no Michi practices the receiving of {{nihongo|divine revelations|神示|shinji}} (お伝え), in which a {{nihongo|spirit medium|神伝え人|}} under divine possession works together with a spirit mediator known as a {{nihongo|''saniwa''|審神者}} (サニワ), along with various ritual assistants<ref>{{cite book |last=Hagiwara |first=Shinmei 萩原真明 |date=2000 |title=Makoto no Michi shinji (vol. 2) 真の道神示 第二集 |location=Tokyo |publisher=Makoto no Michi Publishing Department 真の道出版部 |page=292 |language=ja}}</ref> (''see also'' [[chinkon kishin]]).<ref>{{cite web | title=神示 | website=宗教法人真の道 - 那須に中心の宮を置く古神道 | date=2018-12-31 | url=https://www.makoto.or.jp/archives/64/ | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-03}}</ref>
===Geography=== Makoto no Michi's sacred geography is derived from that of [[Oomoto]] founder [[Onisaburo Deguchi]]'s ''[[Reikai Monogatari]]'', in which Japan is viewed as a {{nihongo|model|雛形|hinagata}} or microcosm of the world. [[Hokkaido]] is viewed as the equivalent of North America, [[Honshu]] as Eurasia, [[Shikoku]] as Australia, [[Kyushu]] as Africa, and [[Taiwan]] as South America; these equivalences stem from their common mythical origins during the creation of the world.<ref name="Shinji2">{{cite book |last=Hagiwara |first=Shinmei 萩原真明 |date=2000 |title=Makoto no Michi shinji (vol. 2) 真の道神示 第二集 |location=Tokyo |publisher=Makoto no Michi Publishing Department 真の道出版部 |pages=250-1 |language=ja}}</ref> The geographic equivalents of the main Japanese islands and Taiwan with the world's continents are identical in both Oomoto<ref>{{cite web | last=Roberts | first=Bill | title=Hokkaido Journal | website=大本公式サイト/ Oomoto Official Site | date=2010-05-20 | url=https://www.oomoto.or.jp/English/enVisitor/enBill7.html | access-date=2025-06-04}}</ref> and Makoto no Michi. For comparison, Oomoto's headquarters in [[Ayabe, Kyoto|Ayabe]] has a garden with islands that represent the same set of islands and continents.<ref>{{cite web | title=金竜海 | website=オニペディア | date=2019-11-18 | url=https://onipedia.info/wiki/%E9%87%91%E7%AB%9C%E6%B5%B7 | language=ja | access-date=2025-06-05}}</ref>
For mountains and lakes, equivalents include:<ref name="Shinji2"/> *''[[Hokkaido]] – [[North America]]'' **[[Daisetsuzan Volcanic Group|Daisetsuzan]] – [[Rocky Mountains]] *''[[Honshu]] – [[Eurasia]]'' **[[Lake Towada]] – [[Lake Baikal]] **[[Mount Gassan]] – [[Central Siberian Plateau]] **[[Lake Suwa]] – [[Lake Manasarovar]] / [[Mount Kailash]] **[[Mount Kisokoma]] – [[Mount Sinai]] **[[Mount Fuji]] – [[Himalayas]] / [[Mount Everest]] *''[[Shikoku]] – [[Australia (continent)|Australia]]'' **[[Mount Tsurugi (Tokushima)|Mount Tsurugi]] – [[Uluru]] *''[[Kyushu]] – [[Africa]]'' **[[Mount Aso]] – [[Kilimanjaro]]'' *''[[Taiwan]] – [[South America]]'' **[[Yu Shan]] – [[Aconcagua]]
==Locations== Makoto no Michi's administrative headquarters is in [[Kasuya, Tokyo]]. The religion also has a [[misogi|waterfall meditation]] site at Nigitama-no-miya (和魂宮; also called ''Tsuki-miya'' 月宮) just north of [[Mount Takao]]. Makoto no Michi's "inner temple" (''Oku-miya'' 奥宮) is Naohi-no-miya (直毘宮), located in [[Nasu, Tochigi]].<ref>{{cite web | title=概要・地図 | website=宗教法人真の道 - 那須に中心の宮を置く古神道 | date=2018-10-26 | url=https://www.makoto.or.jp/overview/ | language=ja | access-date=2025-05-06}}</ref>
==Books== Makoto no Michi has published about a dozen books, a few of which include:<ref>{{cite web | title=書籍 | website=宗教法人真の道 - 那須に中心の宮を置く古神道 | date=2018-10-26 | url=https://www.makoto.or.jp/book/ | language=ja | access-date=2025-05-06}}</ref>
*''Makoto no Michi shinji'' / 真の道神示 (1972) *''Makoto no Michi shinji: vol. 2'' / 真の道神示 第二集 (2000)
Other books include: *真を求めて:萩原真自伝 *飛翔:萩原真先生を偲ぶ *大峰老仙:わしはピヨピヨじゃったよ *守護霊様と私（第二集） *真手入門 *天源 *車に乗った巫女
Books in the ''Makoto no Michi o motomete'' (まことの道を求めて) series: #天命が見える #梶さんの霊界通信 #人間の幸福
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== *{{Official website|https://www.makoto.or.jp/}} {{in lang|ja}}
